sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Liverpool are unbeaten in their last 4 matches, showing resilience with a mix of wins and draws, including a 3-3 thriller against Leeds. Brighton & Hove Albion have had mixed results, with 2 wins, 2 losses, and a draw in their last 5, but their attacking intent remains evident, scoring in 4 of those games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Recent head-to-head encounters suggest high-scoring affairs, with over 2.5 goals in the last 8 meetings and both teams scoring in the last 6. Brighton edged Liverpool 3-2 in their most recent clash in May 2025, while Liverpool secured a 2-1 victory at Anfield in November 2024.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Liverpool, Mohamed Salah and Hugo Ekitiké are pivotal with their goal-scoring prowess (4 and 5 goals respectively this season). Brighton’s Danny Welbeck, with 7 goals, and Jan Paul van Hecke, in fine form with a 7.58 average rating in the last 5 matches, will be crucial.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Liverpool dominate possession (61.4% average) and excel in final third entries (ranked 1st in the league), which could pressure Brighton's defense. Brighton, however, are dangerous on the counter, with a tendency to score late (high percentage of goals in the 76-90th minute range).

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams are level on 23 points, with Liverpool at 10th and Brighton at 8th. Liverpool's 24 goals scored and conceded highlight inconsistency, while Brighton's 25 goals scored show attacking flair, though they've also let in 21.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Liverpool's home advantage at Anfield and Sofascore's 51% probability for a home win, they are slight favorites. However, Brighton's ability to score against Liverpool in recent meetings and their current form suggest a tight contest, likely ending in a narrow victory or draw.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Liverpool lead Brighton & Hove Albion 1-0 at Anfield. The home side struck early through Hugo Ekitiké, setting the tone with a goal in the very first minute. Liverpool have dominated possession and shown more control in the game, while Brighton have had their moments but failed to convert a key opportunity. Both teams have created big chances, but Liverpool's efficiency in front of goal has made the difference so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Liverpool have been the more composed side, controlling the ball for the majority of the first half and creating consistent entries into the final third. Their defense, anchored by Virgil van Dijk and Ibrahima Konaté, has been solid, winning a high percentage of duels and making crucial clearances. Brighton, on the other hand, have shown resilience despite lower possession, managing to penetrate Liverpool’s penalty area multiple times. However, their inability to capitalize on a big chance and a lower duel success rate has left them trailing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: Hugo Ekitiké has been pivotal for Liverpool, scoring the only goal of the half and posing a constant threat up front. Mohamed Salah and Joe Gomez have also played significant roles in creating opportunities, with Gomez providing the assist for the goal. For Brighton, Yankuba Minteh has been a creative spark, setting up a big chance, while Georginio Rutter has been active in midfield with key passes. However, Diego Gómez’s missed opportunity could prove costly for the visitors.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The game began with an early attacking spell from Brighton, but Liverpool quickly took control with sustained pressure in the opening minutes, culminating in their goal. The home side maintained dominance with frequent waves of attack throughout the first half, especially in the latter stages. Brighton showed bursts of intent, particularly in a strong spell midway through the half, pushing forward with intent, but their momentum faded as Liverpool regained control towards the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Liverpool are likely to continue leveraging their possession and home advantage to extend their lead. Brighton will need to sharpen their finishing and capitalize on their penalty area entries to get back into the game. The away side may push for an equalizer early on, but they’ll have to be wary of Liverpool’s counter-attacking threat. Expect a competitive half, with both teams having the potential to find the net if they exploit their chances.
